subtitle: Browse deep dives into Best Paper / Best Student Paper award papers by research area

processing_policy.writing: Paper2Html academic blog; Socratic question→guidance→answer→takeaway; figures are evidence, not decoration
processing_policy.figures: High-resolution local PNG extracted from paper PDF, supplementary PDF, or official project/home pages
processing_policy.formula_check: No ASCII control chars, no Math input error literal, balanced $$, local image paths verified

papers.title: Efficiently Reconstructing Dynamic Scenes One D4RT at a Time
papers.authors: Chuhan Zhang; Guillaume Le Moing; Skanda Koppula; Ignacio Rocco; Liliane Momeni; Junyu Xie; Shuyang Sun; Rahul Sukthankar; Joëlle K Barral; Raia Hadsell; Zoubin Ghahramani; Andrew Zisserman; Junlin Zhang; Mehdi S. M. Sajjadi

papers.title: SAM 3D: 3Dfy Anything in Images

papers.title: ChordEdit: One-Step Low-Energy Transport for Image Editing
papers.authors: Liangsi Lu; Xuhang Chen; Minzhe Guo; Shichu Li; Jingchao Wang; Yang Shi
papers.notes: ChordEdit deep-dive page with PDF/Supp/project figures and MathJax formula preflight.
papers.topic: Image Editing · Transport / One-step T2I Editing
papers.blurb: Uses a low-energy Chord Control Field instead of naive drift difference to achieve training-free, inversion-free one-step real-time image editing.

papers.title: B³-Seg: Camera-Free, Training-Free 3DGS Segmentation via Analytic EIG and Beta–Bernoulli Bayesian Updates
papers.blurb: Recasts training-free / camera-free 3DGS segmentation as Beta–Bernoulli Bayesian updates + analytic EIG active view selection, and proves greedy view selection has a (1−1/e) approximate optimality bound.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate 3DGS Segmentation Bayesian EIG submodular training-free camera-free
papers.authors: Hiromichi Kamata; Samuel Arthur Munro; Fuminori Homma

papers.title: 4D-RGPT: Toward Region-level 4D Understanding via Perceptual Distillation
papers.blurb: Uses Perceptual 4D Distillation to distill frozen 4D experts' latent + explicit representations into the MLLM backbone, with timestamp encoding and region-level prompts, for depth-aware 4D video QA; also introduces R4D-Bench.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Multimodal LLM 4D VQA distillation NVIDIA region-level depth temporal R4D-Bench
papers.authors: Chiao-An Yang; Ryo Hachiuma; Sifei Liu; Subhashree Radhakrishnan; Raymond A. Yeh; Yu-Chiang Frank Wang; Min-Hung Chen

papers.title: tttLRM: Test-Time Training for Long Context and Autoregressive 3D Reconstruction
papers.blurb: Replaces cross-view memory from attention KV cache with gradient-updated fast weights (TTT/LaCT), achieving linear complexity, long context (64 views), high resolution, and autoregressive streaming feedforward 3DGS reconstruction.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate 3D Reconstruction LRM test-time training TTT LaCT fast weights long context autoregressive Adobe
papers.authors: Chen Wang; Hao Tan; Wang Yifan; Zhiqin Chen; Yuheng Liu; Kalyan Sunkavalli; Sai Bi; Lingjie Liu; Yiwei Hu

papers.title: MacTok: Robust Continuous Tokenization for Image Generation
papers.blurb: Uses random + DINO semantic mask reconstruction and global/local representation alignment to prevent posterior collapse in few-token continuous tokenizers, reaching ImageNet SOTA gFID with just 64/128 token.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Image Tokenizer generation posterior collapse masking DINO alignment 1D continuous VAE Fudan
papers.authors: Guanghao Li; Yuxiang Yan; Xin Gao; Hengyu Zeng; Jiaoyang Ruan; Junpeng Ma; Haoyu Albert Wang; Jian Pu

papers.title: A Frame is Worth One Token: Efficient Generative World Modeling with Delta Tokens
papers.blurb: Uses Best-of-Many to produce diverse futures in a single forward pass, then compresses adjacent-frame VFM feature differences into one delta token (video 3D→1D, 1024× compression), making the generative world model both diverse and cheap.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate world model video Best-of-Many delta token VFM feature space mode averaging Amazon generative
papers.authors: Tommie Kerssies; Gabriele Berton; Ju He; Qihang Yu; Wufei Ma; Daan de Geus; Gijs Dubbelman; Liang-Chieh Chen

papers.title: MSPT: Efficient Large-Scale Physical Modeling via Parallelized Multi-Scale Attention
papers.blurb: Uses a ball-tree to split spatially continuous patch, applies local attention within patch + global attention over pooled supernode, keeps complexity dominated by the linear term, and scales single-GPU physical simulation to million-point settings with SOTA accuracy.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Neural PDE physics multi-scale attention ball tree supernode pooling efficient transformer million points Amsterdam
papers.authors: Pedro M. P. Curvo; Jan-Willem van de Meent; Maksim Zhdanov

papers.title: SAM 3D Body: Robust Full-Body Human Mesh Recovery
papers.blurb: Shared encoder + separate body/hand dual decoders disentangle optimization conflicts, while wrist + elbow re-prompt stitches the result back into a consistent full body; with a VLM data engine and multi-stage mesh-fitting labels (700 ten-thousand images), it becomes the first single model that works robustly for both body and hands.

papers.title: LLaDA-MedV: Exploring Large Language Diffusion Models for Biomedical Image Understanding
papers.topic: Medical AI · Masked Diffusion Language VLM
papers.blurb: The first biomedical masked diffusion VLM: it iteratively fills in a fixed-length fully masked canvas, making length an input hyperparameter rather than EOS early stopping; it leads ARM in open-ended dialogue and achieves three consecutive SOTA results on closed-ended VQA (84.93/92.31/95.15).

papers.title: VoxTell: Free-Text Promptable Universal 3D Medical Image Segmentation
papers.topic: Medical AI · Text-Prompted 3D Medical Segmentation / Multi-Scale Vision-Language Fusion
papers.blurb: Changes text fusion from MaskFormer's 'single final dot product' to 'fusion at every decoding scale + deep supervision': trained on 62K+ CT/MRI/PET and 1087 concepts, on 11 unseen datasets it reaches zero-shot average Dice 70.85 (runner-up SAT 51.23), and 50.2 under full-sentence prompts from real radiology reports while SAT gets only 0.0.

papers.title: Scaling Self-Supervised and Cross-Modal Pretraining for Volumetric CT Transformers
papers.topic: Medical AI · Volumetric CT Foundation Model / Two-Level 3D Transformer + Vision-Language Alignment
papers.blurb: The first pure-transformer volumetric CT foundation model: it splits otherwise unaffordable 3D global attention into 'local ViT 24-layer close inspection inside windows (cost linear in the number of windows) + global ViT 4-layer full attention over window descriptors (cheap because G≪m)', with anisotropic patch / 3D RoPE, then performs DINOv3-style self-supervision followed by SigLIP alignment to radiology reports. Using only public CT data, 229,619 sequences, across 6 biomarker benchmarks it ranks first in 4/6 frozen-kNN tests and reaches CT-RATE zero-shot retrieval Recall@5 17.5 (CT-CLIP only 2.9).
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI volumetric CT foundation model 3D ViT two-level attention windowed attention 3D RoPE anisotropic tokenization DINOv3 SigLIP self-supervised vision-language alignment SEoMT segmentation zero-shot retrieval open-data TU Eindhoven
papers.authors: Cris Claessens; Christiaan Viviers; Giacomo D'Amicantonio; Egor Bondarev; Fons van der Sommen
papers.missing_info: Total GPU count/wall-clock time is not disclosed in the paper (only batch/steps/epoch are given); biomarker AUC has no numeric table in the main text (Figure 3 bar heights are readings from the figure); exact names of individual datasets in Tables 1/3 should follow the appendix

papers.title: X-PCR: A Benchmark for Cross-modality Progressive Clinical Reasoning in Ophthalmic Diagnosis
papers.topic: Medical AI · Ophthalmic Diagnostic Reasoning Benchmark / Six-Stage Dependent Reasoning Chain + Cross-Modal Alignment Evaluation
papers.blurb: Turns ophthalmic diagnosis from 'a pile of independent multiple-choice questions' into 'a six-stage reasoning chain with causal dependencies (IQA→AL→LC→DD→SG→CD) + cross-six-modality alignment reasoning', then adds difficulty stratification and uncertainty-aware (UAS/ECE) scoring: 26,415 expert-verified images, 177,868 VQA, 52 diseases, 51 public datasets. A single Chain Completion Rate exposes the flaw: among 21 MLLMs, the strongest, GPT-5, averages 76.24% on single steps, but only 24.47% across all six steps, and no model exceeds attending physicians (79.91%).
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI benchmark ophthalmic diagnosis MLLM progressive clinical reasoning chain completion rate cross-modal alignment uncertainty-aware UAS ECE calibration VQA six-stage CFP OCT FFA ICGA RetCam external-photography GPT-5 difficulty-stratified Shenzhen University
papers.authors: Gui Wang; Zehao Zhong; YongSong Zhou; Yudong Li; Ende Wu; Wooi Ping Cheah; Rong Qu; Jianfeng Ren; Linlin Shen
papers.missing_info: Exact GPU hours/wall-clock time are not disclosed in the paper (only A100 single-GPU/multi-GPU division of work and zero-shot deterministic decoding are given); Tables 2/3/4 are representative excerpt transcriptions from the original 21-row tables (see original paper for all rows)

papers.title: SurgCoT: Advancing Spatiotemporal Reasoning in Surgical Videos through a Chain-of-Thought Benchmark
papers.topic: Medical AI · Surgical Video Spatiotemporal Reasoning / Chain-of-Thought Benchmark
papers.blurb: The first cross-specialty surgical video CoT reasoning benchmark: it decomposes large spatiotemporal problems into three stages, 'video-level → clip-level → frame-level' (Q1→Q2→Q3, the higher-level Answer conditions the next level), annotates each level with a five-tuple Question→Option→Knowledge→Clue→Answer, and makes reason-then-decide explicit. 7 specialties, 35 procedures, 2,841 videos, 19,345 main questions + 59,177 subquestions, evaluating 10 MLLMs × 5 dimensions × three settings (BL/KE/FC). The sharpest finding: main questions are answered correctly while substeps are broadly wrong (GPT-5 main 87.58% but Q3 only 50.51%, Claude main 87.54% / Q3 30.44%), so high accuracy is often shortcutting rather than real reasoning.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI surgical video benchmark spatiotemporal reasoning chain-of-thought CoT MLLM evaluation five-tuple annotation progressive reasoning Knowledge Clue causal action ordering anomaly onset tracking shortcut learning Shenzhen University CVI-SZU
papers.authors: Gui Wang; YongSong Zhou; Kaijun Deng; Wooi Ping Cheah; Rong Qu; Jianfeng Ren; Linlin Shen
papers.missing_info: Total evaluation time/API cost/whether the full dataset is publicly released are not disclosed in the paper; Figure 3 data distribution has no numeric table in the main text; the main text says 12 models are evaluated while the main table actually reports 10, and open-source model names (Intern3.5-VL-8B / InternVL-8B) are not fully consistent, so this page follows the measured table headers

papers.title: Uni-Hema: Unified Model for Digital Hematopathology
papers.topic: Medical AI · Digital Hematopathology / Unified Multi-Task Multimodal Model
papers.blurb: The first unified vision-language model for digital hematopathology (DHP): a shared backbone (ResNet-50 + DINO-DETR encoder-decoder + T5 text encoder-decoder), with Hema-Former routing 'hierarchical visual features + task-related query' at different granularities: CMF cross-modal fusion (language), TGVR top-K text modulation (detection/morphology), SCFE text-independent single-cell features (classification), and QGMF using Einstein summation to turn lesion query ⊗ pixel features into segmentation logits (pixels). 46 public sets (≈70 ten-thousand images + 22K QA + 7K MLM), single RTX 4090 for about 8 days, one 6-stage progressively frozen joint training run. Detection mean mAP50 61.1, classification mean F1 92.5, morphology 77.2 (AttriDet 62.6), and unseen-domain classification mean F1 90.8 ranks first; the cost is that small-sample tasks are overtaken by single-task models and segmentation edges are slightly blurred.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI digital hematopathology DHP unified model multi-task multi-modal Hema-Former cross-modal fusion text-guided refinement single-cell feature extractor query-guided mask former Einstein summation detection segmentation classification morphology VQA masked language modeling ResNet DINO-DETR T5 Mask DINO progressive training RTX 4090 ITU Punjab Chughtai Lab leukemia malaria anemia sickle cell
papers.authors: Abdul Rehman; Iqra Rasool; Ayesha Imran; Mohsen Ali; Waqas Sultani
papers.missing_info: Details such as Step 2 epoch/batch and each stage's LR/optimizer are in the appendix (supplementary material was not available for this page, marked as not specified in the paper); total training is only reported as 'about 8 days/single 4090', so GPU-hours are a rough estimate; Table 1 contains more dataset rows, and this page keeps representative rows + Mean; CVF page is not online, only arXiv + code links

papers.title: LEMON: A Large Endoscopic MONocular Dataset and Foundation Model for Perception in Surgical Settings
papers.topic: Medical AI · Large Surgical Endoscopy Dataset + Augmented Self-Distillation Foundation Model
papers.blurb: Private surgical data is not enough to feed foundation models, so LEMON switches to a fully public route: crawl about 1.8 ten-thousand videos from YouTube, then use a multi-level curation pipeline (video→frame→region, progressively removing non-surgical content by granularity) to reduce them to 4194 clips/938h/85M frames/35 procedures, making it the largest open surgical dataset to date. The foundation model LemonFM adds an 'augmented distillation' branch on top of DINO self-distillation: nearest-neighbor frames from other people undergoing the same procedure + temporally adjacent frames are also treated as positive views, explicitly training cross-patient/cross-frame invariance (3× distance threshold as a quality knob). Under both linear probing and full fine-tuning protocols, it leads across 4 tasks and 6 datasets: phase Jaccard up to +9.5pp, segmentation +10.3pp mDice, and fine-tuning with 50% labels still beats others using 100%.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI surgical endoscopy dataset foundation model self-supervised DINO knowledge distillation augmented distillation nearest-neighbor mining ConvNeXt linear probing full fine-tuning phase recognition tool detection action recognition semantic segmentation YouTube curation data efficiency King's College London
papers.authors: Chengan Che; Chao Wang; Tom Vercauteren; Sophia Tsoka; Luis C. Garcia-Peraza-Herrera
papers.missing_info: End-to-end pretraining wall-clock time is not disclosed in the paper (only 8×V100, batch, epoch, and per-step training time in Table 12 are given); Figure 1 radar chart values are readings from axis ticks, with exact values governed by Tables 3/4; LEMON data is distributed via YouTube links, so entries fail if original videos are deleted, and exact frame-level reproduction cannot be guaranteed

papers.title: OctoMed: Data Recipes for State-of-the-Art Multimodal Medical Reasoning
papers.topic: Medical AI · Data Recipe for Medical Multimodal Reasoning: Pure SFT Distillation + Rejection-Sampled Multi-Trajectory Data
papers.blurb: While others are busy inventing new losses, stacking RL, and using larger backbone, OctoMed bets everything on 'what data to feed': distilling samples with reasoning trajectories from strong teacher models (DeepSeek-R1 / GPT-4o), using rejection sampling to keep only correct trajectories, and scaling to 800 ten-thousand samples / 68 hundred-million token (currently the largest medical multimodal reasoning dataset). With supervised fine-tuning alone, it tunes a 7B student (Qwen2.5-VL) to cross-distribution open-source SOTA, surpasses its teacher GPT-4o in multimodal classification (67.29 vs 53.96), and overall beats the 4× larger MedGemma-27B. Three mechanisms: multiple correct trajectories per question (1→16) act as regularization, lifting the MedQA peak from 75.16 to 85.01 (more valuable than training extra epoch); CoT helps reasoning, while direct helps perceptual classification; mixing trajectories of different lengths yields 'question-difficulty-calibrated reasoning length' without explicit supervision: long answers for hard questions, short answers for easy ones.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI multimodal medical reasoning data recipe SFT distillation rejection sampling reasoning traces multiple trajectories regularization CoT vs direct prompting emergent task-aware thinking reasoning length self-calibration DeepSeek-R1 GPT-4o teacher Qwen2.5-VL-7B student 8M examples 6.8B tokens OOD generalization knowledge distillation UW-Madison Microsoft Research
papers.authors: Timothy Ossowski; Sheng Zhang; Qianchu Liu; Guanghui Qin; Reuben Tan; Tristan Naumann; Junjie Hu; Hoifung Poon
papers.missing_info: No public training/data code repository (only HF model weights OctoMed/OctoMed-7B are released); total teacher calls, GPU count, wall-clock time, and API cost are not disclosed in the paper, so no compute estimate is made; the exact mixture ratios/sampling quotas/teacher prompt for the 8M dataset are only given as a framework, and item-by-item reproduction would require rebuilding them; Figure 4/Figure 8 were missed by the caption-anchored extractor because captions are tightly attached to the main text, so PyMuPDF page-coordinate cropping was used on the same regions (not blind page rendering); appendix qualitative Figures S1–S3 were not extracted, and the three related properties are paraphrased from the paper text.

papers.title: MedMO: Grounding and Understanding Multimodal Large Language Model for Medical Images
papers.topic: Medical AI · Medical Multimodal Large Model / Four-Stage Post-Training for Understanding + Grounding
papers.blurb: An open-source medical multimodal large model post-trained from Qwen3-VL-8B and using only public data: it separates the data-economics conflict between 'understanding images' and 'boxing lesions' into four-stage progressive post-training: 18.5M low-resolution (768²) data first establishes cross-modal alignment, then resolution rises to 1280² to inject grounding, instruction tuning aligns the QA style, and GRPO reinforcement comes last. The RL stage introduces purely geometric, verifiable bounding-box rewards (diagonal-normalized L1 + GIoU + Hungarian matching + FP/FN penalties), polishing spatial precision without poisoning language. MedMO-8B-Next reaches VQA average 72.7% and grounding six-task average 56.8 (strongest baseline 17.2), and 40.5 IoU on DeepLesion against 0.00 for multiple baselines.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI multimodal LLM MLLM VLM medical grounding bounding box GRPO verifiable reward GIoU Hungarian matching Qwen3-VL post-training curriculum learning resolution escalation visual question answering report generation MedTrinity DeepLesion open-source MBZUAI
papers.authors: Ankan Deria; Komal Kumar; Adinath Madhavrao Dukre; Eran Segal; Salman Khan; Imran Razzak
papers.missing_info: The exact modality proportions in the 26M corpus, Stage 4 λ_FP/λ_FN values, and epoch counts for each stage are not specified in the paper; Tables 1/2 are excerpt transcriptions (see original paper for full per-benchmark values); training GPU-hours are rough estimates based on disclosed numbers

papers.title: Med-CMR: A Fine-Grained Benchmark Integrating Visual Evidence and Clinical Logic for Medical Complex Multimodal Reasoning
papers.topic: Medical AI · Complex Medical Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark / Seven-Dimensional Capability Decomposition + Dual-Track Scoring
papers.blurb: The first medical multimodal benchmark to decompose clinical complex reasoning into fine-grained (seven-dimensional) capabilities: it splits the single overall score of 'medical multimodal reasoning' into 3 visual dimensions (small-object detection/detail discrimination/spatial understanding) + 4 reasoning dimensions (temporal prediction/causal reasoning/long-tail generalization/multi-source integration), with a dual-track protocol of MCQ accuracy + open-ended four-dimensional weighted LLM scoring (visual accuracy/ground-truth correctness each weight 4, consistency/coherence each weight 1). 20,653 questions come from real journal cases, covering 11 systems × 12 modalities, reviewed in two stages by experts + models. 18 models evaluated: GPT-5 ranks first (MCQ 57.81/open 48.70), but medically fine-tuned models are not consistently better than general models, and long-tail generalization is the hardest across the field.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI medical multimodal benchmark MLLM evaluation complex reasoning fine-grained capability decomposition seven dimensions VQA MCQ open-ended LLM-as-Judge long-tail generalization GPT-5 Qwen3-VL clinical reasoning small-object detection causal reasoning temporal prediction multi-source integration NUS Nanjing-University TUM
papers.authors: Haozhen Gong; Xiaozhong Ji; Yuansen Liu; Wenbin Wu; Xiaoxiao Yan; Jingjing Liu; Kai Wu; Jiazhen Pan; Bailiang Jian; Jiangning Zhang; Xiaobin Hu; Hongwei Bran Li
papers.missing_info: Evaluation GPU count/wall-clock time are not disclosed in the paper (pure evaluation with no training, so no GPU-hours estimate is made); no runnable standalone code repository/data download is available (only the project page github.com/LsmnBmnc/Med-CMR was verified); Tables 2/3 are complete 18-row transcriptions, Figure 3 r/p are taken from labels inside the figure, and there is no independent lesion-level mask/bbox visual-grounding verification

papers.title: OraPO: Oracle-educated Reinforcement Learning for Data-efficient and Factual Radiology Report Generation
papers.topic: Medical AI · Data-Efficient Radiology Report Generation / Oracle-Educated GRPO + Fact-Level Rewards
papers.blurb: The first RL algorithm to couple DPO into GRPO: at the start of RRG training, about 30% of GRPO groups have all-0 rewards, advantage≈0, and nearly vanished gradients; OraPO treats these 'failed rollout' samples as ready-made negatives and ground-truth as positives for lightweight DPO, exactly supplying the gradient GRPO lacks. It adaptively anneals between GRPO/DPO by per-prompt Zero-Reward Rate (a 'teach first, then explore' flywheel), with recall-biased FactScore fact-level dense rewards. Single-stage pure RL, 3B Qwen2.5-VL, 1K samples, 4×A10, CheXpert Plus F1 0.341 / Recall 0.832, MIMIC-CXR F1 0.357, using 2–3 orders of magnitude less data than the previous SOTA.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI radiology report generation RRG GRPO DPO direct preference optimization reinforcement learning RL-only FactScore reward zero-reward rate data-efficient chest X-ray CheXpert-Plus MIMIC-CXR Qwen2.5-VL recall clinical effectiveness Oracle-Health University-of-Queensland
papers.authors: Zhuoxiao Chen; Hongyang Yu; Ying Xu; Yadan Luo; Long Duong; Yuan-Fang Li
papers.missing_info: No public code repository (checked arXiv/GitHub; only included in the awesome-RRG list, no official implementation); CVF 2026 proceedings page is not yet online (checked 403); complete hyperparameters/LR/optimizer/steps are said to be in Supplementary and are not fully listed in the main text; total training GPU card-hours/wall-clock time and FactS GPT-4.1 call cost are not disclosed in the paper, so no GPU-hours estimate is made; Tables 1/Table 2 are representative excerpt transcriptions (original has 20+/16 rows), Tables 3/4/5 are complete transcriptions; appendix Figure 3 qualitative example is a full-page text comparison table and was transcribed as a table rather than pasted as an image by convention

papers.title: CURE: Curriculum-guided Multi-task Training for Reliable Anatomy Grounded Report Generation
papers.topic: Medical AI · Reliable Radiology Report Generation / Error-Aware Curriculum + Anatomy-Level Report Reconstruction
papers.blurb: Adds no new data and changes only the training methodology: it reconstructs finding-generation into region-by-region AGRG supervising both normal and abnormal findings (pulling the abnormal base rate back from phrase-grounding's ≈1 to the real value), then uses an error-normalized curriculum p_i=e_i/∑e_j to dynamically steer scarce sampling budget toward weak spots. A LoRA-tuned MedGemma-4B: anatomy-level localization IoU 0.249→0.601 (doubles), six-region abnormality hallucination 26.5%→8.78% (−18.6pt).
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI radiology report generation RRG visual grounding anatomy-grounded AGRG error-aware curriculum learning self-paced learning hallucination MedGemma-4B LoRA MAIRA-2 Chest ImaGenome MS-CXR PadChest-GR VinDr-CXR public-data-only
papers.authors: Pablo Messina; Andrés Villa; Juan León Alcázar; Karen Sánchez; Carlos Hinojosa; Denis Parra; Álvaro Soto; Bernard Ghanem
papers.missing_info: GPU count and wall-clock time are not disclosed in the paper (only LoRA r16/4-bit, effective batch 25, and 9000 steps are given); adaptive α values and complete columns for Tables 5/Table 7 are in the appendix (main text excerpts representative columns and notes that full tables are in the original paper)

papers.title: IBISAgent: Reinforcing Pixel-Level Visual Reasoning in MLLMs for Universal Biomedical Object Referring and Segmentation
papers.topic: Medical AI · Pixel-Level Visual Reasoning Agent / Recasting Segmentation as a Multi-Step MDP
papers.blurb: Does not modify the MLLM architecture or introduce an implicit <SEG> token: it recasts biomedical segmentation as a multi-step Markov decision process. The model alternates between emitting <think> reasoning and <action> textual click coordinates (Target,±1,Coordinate2d), calls frozen MedSAM2 to turn clicks into masks, overlays them semi-transparently back onto the original image as observations, and iteratively refines one click at a time. Two-stage training: cold-start SFT(456K automatically simulated click trajectories) + GRPO(no KL) RL with five types of fine-grained rule rewards (format/answer/region click placement: positive points land on FN, negative points land on FP/progressive IoU monotonically non-decreasing/trajectory length). Base Qwen2.5-VL-7B, 16×A100. It leads across all three test sets in IoU (in-domain 85.58, out-of-domain 80.63, external-hospital 72.09, best opponent only 50.74); removing fine-grained rewards collapses IoU to 11.77; the cost is ~3× inference latency.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI agentic MLLM pixel-level visual reasoning biomedical segmentation referring segmentation reasoning segmentation mask refinement Markov decision process MDP cold-start SFT GRPO reinforcement learning fine-grained rule reward region-based click placement progressive IoU reward MedSAM2 tool-augmented Qwen2.5-VL BiomedParseData decoupled reasoning cross-domain generalization Zhejiang University Shanghai AI Lab
papers.authors: Yankai Jiang; Qiaoru Li; Binlu Xu; Haoran Sun; Chao Ding; Junting Dong; Yuxiang Cai; Xuhong Zhang; Jianwei Yin
papers.missing_info: Total GPU hours/wall-clock time are not disclosed in the paper (only 16×A100, SFT 10ep/RL 12ep, batch 256 are given); the main text does not list a complete VQA numeric table (none was invented); external-hospital held-out data comes from three private medical centers and is not public, so that result cannot be directly reproduced by third parties; the code repository exists but README releases are marked TODO/partial, making end-to-end reproduction relatively hard

papers.title: CARE: A Molecular-Guided Foundation Model with Adaptive Region Modeling for Whole Slide Image Analysis
papers.topic: Medical AI · Pathology Whole-Slide Foundation Model / Adaptive Regions + Molecular-Guided Alignment
papers.blurb: Changes WSI 'how to cut regions' from a fixed grid to learnable soft routing: soft inclusion assignments + top-3 candidates + 'semantic×spatial' score-based reassignment produce morphologically coherent adaptive regions (like word-level token in NLP). Region Structuring Loss pulls the 'expected ranking' toward E*=0.5 to resist collapse, SPF pools WSI/ROI representations with 'coverage prior ⊕ gated semantics', and a molecular-guided curriculum follows iBOT self-supervision→RNA→protein InfoNCE. With only about 1/10 data, 34,277 WSI, it leads on 33-task average molecular prediction LR 69.5 and survival C-index 58.0, while matching the strongest TITAN on morphology classification.

papers.title: Molmo2: Open Weights and Data for Vision-Language Models with Video Understanding and Grounding
papers.blurb: A SoTA video-language model family with open weights, data, and code, without distilling closed-source models: 9 new datasets + packing/message-tree (×15 training) + token weighting extend 2D pointing into space-time, and video pointing/tracking surpasses Gemini 3 Pro.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Vision-Language Model video understanding grounding pointing tracking open weights open data no distillation packing message-tree token weighting AllenAI
papers.authors: Christopher Clark; Jieyu Zhang; Zixian Ma; Jae Sung Park; Mohammadreza Salehi; Rohun Tripathi; Sangho Lee; Ali Farhadi; Ranjay Krishna

papers.title: MatAnyone 2: Scaling Video Matting via a Learned Quality Evaluator
papers.blurb: Uses a Matting Quality Evaluator (MQE) that needs no ground-truth alpha to classify each pixel as reliable/error, serving both as online training supervision and offline data arbiter. It fuses video matting (semantically stable) and image matting (boundary-friendly) to build VMReal, a real dataset with 28K clips / 2.4M frames, and adds a reference-frame strategy to handle appearance changes in long videos. Pure CNN, only the first-frame mask is needed, and it ranks first on all metrics across synthetic and real benchmarks.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Video Matting learned quality evaluator MQE memory propagation reference frame real-world dataset VMReal DINOv3 alpha matting NTU SenseTime
papers.authors: Peiqing Yang; Shangchen Zhou; Kai Hao; Qingyi Tao

papers.title: VGGT-Segmentor: Geometry-Enhanced Cross-View Segmentation
papers.blurb: Finds that VGGT's pixelwise point projection systematically drifts in ego–exo cross-view segmentation, while object-level attention remains stably aligned. Based on this, it freezes VGGT and adds only a lightweight Union Segmentation Head (Mask Prompt Fusion builds region consensus → Point-Guided Prediction demotes drifting points into sparse prompt → Mask Refinement iteratively cleans boundaries), then removes paired-label requirements through single-image self-supervised training. On Ego–Exo4D, 67.7/68.0 IoU sets SOTA, and the zero-paired-label pretrained version surpasses the previous fully supervised SOTA DOMR.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Segmentation Cross-View VGGT geometry-enhanced Ego-Exo4D Union Segmentation Head point projection drift mask refinement self-supervised single-image Beihang
papers.authors: Yulu Gao; Bohao Zhang; Zongheng Tang; Jitong Liao; Wenjun Wu; Si Liu

papers.title: MuM: Multi-View Masked Image Modeling for 3D Vision
papers.blurb: Extends MAE's unified masking + pixel reconstruction unchanged to arbitrary multi-view images of the same scene: all views are masked 75%, a shared-weight ViT-L encoder encodes each frame independently, and a lightweight ViT-B decoder with cross-frame attention reconstructs jointly: symmetric, no reference frame, and n=1 automatically degenerates to MAE. Geometry is compressed into the encoder as a byproduct of 'doing multi-view reconstruction under high masking'. Using only about DINOv3 1/30-scale compute, MuM as a frozen feature encoder surpasses DINOv3 and CroCo v2 on almost all geometry tasks such as multi-view three-dimensional reconstruction, dense matching, and relative pose (semantic tasks still lag DINOv3).
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate 3D Vision Multi-View Masked Image Modeling MIM MAE self-supervised representation geometric features CroCo DINOv3 dense matching feedforward reconstruction relative pose ViT RoPE Chalmers
papers.authors: David Nordström; Johan Edstedt; Fredrik Kahl; Georg Bökman

papers.title: V²-SAM: Marrying SAM2 with Multi-Prompt Experts for Cross-View Object Correspondence
papers.blurb: SAM2 needs 'coordinates in the target view' to segment, but under cross-view settings that coordinate is exactly unknown. V²-SAM uses two complementary prompt generators to 'create' it: V²-Anchor uses DINOv3 geometric features for cross-view matching→foreground constraint→sparse hierarchy→centroid, unlocking cross-view coordinate prompts for SAM2 for the first time (entirely non-trainable); V²-Visual uses VPMatcher to map appearance through both feature and structure paths; then three experts, Anchor/Visual/Fusion, plus the point-level cycle-consistency selector PCCS choose the most reliable prediction per instance. Ego-Exo4D 48.0 total IoU, DAVIS-17 78.8 J&F, and HANDAL-X 77.2 IoU are SOTA on all three leaderboards, with only 15M trainable parameters (about 1% of ObjectRelator).
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate SAM2 DINOv3 cross-view correspondence object tracking Ego-Exo Ego-Exo4D multi-prompt experts cyclic consistency PCCS coordinate prompt segmentation HANDAL-X DAVIS INSAIT Tsinghua Fudan
papers.authors: Jiancheng Pan; Runze Wang; Tianwen Qian; Yanwei Fu; Xiangyang Xue; Xiaomeng Huang; Luc Van Gool; Danda Pani Paudel; Yuqian Fu

papers.title: INSID3: Training-Free In-Context Segmentation with DINOv3
papers.blurb: One annotated example is expected to segment any concept in any domain and at any granularity: this simultaneously needs cross-image semantic matching and in-image region grouping, so prior work either fine-tunes VFM (hurting generalization) or stitches DINOv2+SAM together (bulky and locked in granularity). INSID3 finds that a frozen DINOv3 already has both capabilities; the only blocker is a systematic positional bias (patches at the same coordinates in different images show spuriously high similarity regardless of semantics). It estimates a low-dimensional position subspace from noise images and removes it with training-free orthogonal projection; then raw features do agglomerative clustering (in-image grouping), debiased features do reverse correspondence + cross-image similarity seed selection, and finally cross-image semantics × in-image self-similarity multiplicative aggregation completes the mask. 9 benchmarks across four domains reach SOTA across the board, average +7.5% mIoU, only 304M (about 1/3 of the SAM pipeline), zero mask supervision; transferring debiasing to semantic correspondence adds another +6.6% PCK.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate training-free in-context segmentation DINOv3 feature matching positional debiasing orthogonal projection one-shot segmentation part segmentation personalized segmentation self-supervised VFM agglomerative clustering semantic correspondence SPair-71k COCO LVIS PASCAL-Part Oral
papers.authors: Claudia Cuttano; Gabriele Trivigno; Christoph Reich; Daniel Cremers; Carlo Masone; Stefan Roth

papers.title: Real-World Point Tracking with Verifier-Guided Pseudo-Labeling
papers.blurb: Long-horizon point tracking is almost entirely trained on synthetic data, and performance drops on real videos due to the sim-to-real domain gap. Self-training with pseudo-labels is the way out, but a single teacher's reliability fluctuates frame by frame, different teachers' errors are weakly correlated, and naive random selection/averaging amplifies noise into drift. This work redefines the problem as framewise reliability estimation and proposes verifier, a meta-model that does not directly predict coordinates: it uses frozen CoTracker3 features to compare the query's local appearance with each candidate's local appearance; through Candidate Transformer (restricted cross-attn + temporal self-attn), it outputs a framewise candidate reliability distribution, trained with a soft contrastive target from the negative softmax of candidate-to-GT distances, requiring only synthetic labels throughout. verifier can serve both as inference ensembling and as guidance for real-world fine-tuning. Base Track-On2 → Track-On-R: EgoPoints, RoboTAP, Kinetics, and DAVIS all reach SOTA (EgoPoints δ_avg 61.7→67.3), while using less data.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate point tracking TAP track any point verifier verifier-guided pseudo-labeling self-training teacher ensemble Track-On-R Track-On2 CoTracker3 BootsTAPIR EgoPoints RoboTAP DAVIS Kinetics reliability estimation sim-to-real Koc University Shanghai Jiao Tong
papers.authors: Görkay Aydemir; Fatma Güney; Weidi Xie

papers.title: AsymLoc: Towards Asymmetric Feature Matching for Efficient Visual Localization
papers.blurb: The compute budgets at the two ends of visual localization are inherently asymmetric: map-library images are built offline and can freely use large models, while query images must be processed in real time on edge devices such as smart glasses. AsymLoc therefore proposes asymmetric visual localization: the map side uses a large teacher T to extract features offline, the query side uses a student S as small as 0.04M parameters to extract features online, and zero-parameter mutual nearest-neighbor matching aligns the two sides. The challenge is that features from two different models are naturally incompatible, and adding a 13M rematcher such as LightGlue is undesirable. AsymLoc puts alignment into the joint detector-descriptor space: geometric matching loss L_match anchors spatial correspondences via known homographies, and joint detector-descriptor distillation loss L_KD uses row/column bidirectional KL to make the student replicate the teacher's joint matchability distribution (aligning relations, not vectors). Under a SiLK teacher, a 0.13M student reaches 95.5% of teacher accuracy on Aachen with an order of magnitude less inference compute; a 25× smaller student still preserves 96%+ teacher accuracy, beating symmetric baselines and asymmetric distillation methods adapted from image retrieval across the board.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Visual Localization Asymmetric Feature Matching detector descriptor knowledge distillation mutual nearest neighbor camera pose 6-DoF retrieval edge on-device SuperPoint SiLK LightGlue HPatches ScanNet IMC2022 Aachen UT-Austin Amazon
papers.authors: Mohammad Omama; Gabriele Berton; Eric Foxlin; Yelin Kim

papers.title: Retrieve and Segment: Are a Few Examples Enough to Bridge the Supervision Gap in Open-Vocabulary Segmentation?
papers.blurb: Open-vocabulary segmentation lags fully supervised methods because VLMs have only image-level supervision and language itself is ambiguous. RNS gives each class a few pixel-labeled images as visual support, turning zero-shot into few-shot; the core question becomes how to fuse text support and visual support. Unlike the late manual addition in kNN-CLIP / FREEDA, RNS first fuses the two modalities at the prototype level as f=λt+(1-λ)v, then uses retrieval (for each query patch, take k nearest neighbors from visual support) to assemble an on-the-fly training set for each test image and train a lightweight linear classifier per-image on A100 in <1 second. Three independent validations: Figure 5 retrieval ablation, Table 1 component removal, and Figures 3/4 full/partial support. DINOv3.txt+SAM with 20 images per class narrows the gap to full supervision to 11.5 mIoU, +34 over zero-shot, using only about 964 annotations (full supervision uses 20k).
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate open-vocabulary segmentation retrieval-augmented few-shot test-time adaptation test-time training modality fusion prototype fusion VLM CLIP DINOv3 SAM zero-shot personalized segmentation Highlight
papers.authors: Tilemachos Aravanis; Vladan Stojnić; Bill Psomas; Nikos Komodakis; Giorgos Tolias

papers.title: MAMMA: Markerless Accurate Multi-person Motion Acquisition
papers.blurb: Marker-based systems such as Vicon are the gold standard for motion capture, but they require reflective markers and manual segment-by-segment cleanup of noisy markers, and they especially fail when multiple people are in close contact. MAMMA upgrades supervision from sparse keypoints to 512 dense 2D surface landmarks: ViT + mask-CNN + a transformer decoder with one learnable query per landmark (MammaNet) outputs landmark position/uncertainty/visibility/contact for every frame and view; then SAM2 mask conditioning + symmetric epipolar distance performs 100% cross-view person association, and finally multi-view reprojection fits SMPL-X without pose priors or initialization. It is trained entirely on synthetic data MammaSyn (BEDLAM expanded to 32 cameras + interaction/hands). Hi4D 19-joint MPJPE 12.44mm (previous SOTA AvatarPose 32.10), held-out marker error against Vicon+MoSh is only 0.862mm, animation is visually indistinguishable, and the full capture-to-SMPL-X pipeline drops from about 72 hours to 26 hours.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate markerless mocap motion capture multi-person multi-view keypoints dense 2D landmarks surface landmarks SMPL-X SMPL pose estimation triangulation epipolar correspondence SAM2 mask conditioning landmark queries transformer decoder BEDLAM MammaSyn Vicon Harmony4D Hi4D CHI3D MOYO contact reprojection optimization Max Planck CMU
papers.authors: Hanz Cuevas-Velasquez; Anastasios Yiannakidis; Soyong Shin; Giorgio Becherini; Markus Höschle; Joachim Tesch; Taylor Obersat; Tsvetelina Alexiadis; Eni Halilaj; Michael J. Black

papers.title: The SA-FARI Dataset: Segment Anything in Footage of Animals for Recognition and Identification
papers.topic: Multi-Animal Tracking Dataset · Camera Trap Video Segmentation
papers.blurb: Multi-animal tracking (MAT) for wildlife is the foundation for species/individual/behavior recognition, but it has not taken off in ten years. The bottleneck is not the model but the dataset: existing datasets almost always sacrifice one of scale (<1h annotated), species diversity (≤5 species), spatiotemporal diversity (a few days in one protected area), or annotation quality (masks mostly auto-generated, no human verification). SA-FARI does all four mutually exclusive things at once: 741 independent sampling sites, 4 continents, 10 years (2014–2024), 99 species, 11,609 camera-trap videos, producing 16,224 frame-by-frame human-verified masklets and 942,702 boxes/masks (about 46 hours): 5× total duration and 2× species diversity over any previous MAT dataset, and the first large-scale wild MAT dataset with human-verified spatiotemporal segmentation. The key mechanism uses the SAM 3 data engine's human-AI-in-the-loop process (automatic pseudo-labeling→dual annotators + SAM2 in-loop correction→exhaustiveness checks) to reduce exhaustive annotation cost. Two independent validations: feeding the training set to SOTA models raises HOTA by 20+ points, and fine-tuning SAM 3 gives a threefold boost (training value); slicing the test set by small-mask/challenging/multi/night exposes failure modes: small targets hurt detection, and occlusion/motion hurts association (diagnostic value).
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate dataset benchmark multi-animal tracking MAT video segmentation SAM SAM 3 camera trap wildlife conservation animal masklet tracking spatio-temporal segmentation open-vocabulary HOTA long-tail re-identification Conservation X Labs Meta
papers.authors: Dante Francisco Wasmuht; Otto Brookes; Maximillian Schall; Pablo Palencia; Chris Beirne; Tilo Burghardt; Majid Mirmehdi; Hjalmar Kühl; Mimi Arandjelovic; Sam Pottie; Peter Bermant; Brandon Asheim; Yi Jin Toh; Adam Elzinga; Jason Holmberg; Andrew Whitworth; Eleanor Flatt; Laura Gustafson; Chaitanya Ryali; Yuan-Ting Hu; Baishan Guo; Andrew Westbury; Kate Saenko; Didac Surís

papers.title: MARCO: Navigating the Unseen Space of Semantic Correspondence
papers.topic: Semantic Correspondence · Single DINOv2 Backbone + Densified Self-Distillation
papers.blurb: The mainstream semantic correspondence recipe is a DINOv2+Stable Diffusion dual encoder, accurate but close to 10 hundred-million parameters, and a model trained with sparse keypoints (≤20 points per image) collapses when switched to unseen keypoints/categories (Fig.2b: supervision shrinks the dense matchable flow across the image down around annotated points). MARCO uses only a single DINOv2 backbone (+ adapter + upsampling head with <5% parameters) and fills this gap with a training framework: first, coarse-to-fine Gaussian RBF distribution matching, with cosine annealing shrinking kernel width σ from 3 to 1, first aligning regions and then enforcing subpatch-level precision, avoiding soft-argmax L2 being pulled to the mean under multimodality; second, dense self-distillation via flow anchoring: an EMA teacher mines mutual-nearest-neighbor seeds, Delaunay triangulation + piecewise-affine warp densify them into a dense displacement field, then clusters in displacement space and uses GT keypoint anchor filtering for symmetry/occlusion mismatches, turning a handful of keypoints into thousands of reliable pseudo-correspondences so sparse supervision covers the whole object surface. SPair-71k/AP-10K/PF-PASCAL reach SOTA across the board, strictest threshold PCK@0.01 +8.9, unseen-keypoint SPair-U +5.1, unseen-category MP-100 +4.7, while being 3× smaller and 10× faster than diffusion dual encoders. Ablation ladder (SPair-U @0.10): no dense loss 41.8→MNN 52.5→+Delaunay 64.7→+GT anchor 67.5.
papers.tags: Top Paper candidate Semantic Correspondence feature matching DINOv2 generalization unseen keypoints unseen categories self-distillation EMA teacher coarse-to-fine Gaussian RBF dense pseudo-labels mutual nearest neighbor Delaunay triangulation piecewise-affine warp flow anchoring SPair-71k AP-10K PF-PASCAL MP-100 SPair-U single-backbone Politecnico di Torino TU Darmstadt hessian.AI

papers.title: Better Tokens for Better 3D: Advancing Vision-Language Modeling in 3D Medical Imaging
papers.topic: Medical AI · 3D Medical Imaging VLM Tokenizer / Wavelet Frequency Domain + Causal Convolution + LFQ + Three-Stage Curriculum
papers.blurb: Repositions the bottleneck of 3D medical VLM from 'language backbone size' to 'fidelity and scalability of voxel token': 3D Haar wavelet frequency-domain compression + causal 3D convolution + lookup-free quantization + three-stage curriculum generalize to >300 slices without increasing memory, with report generation clinical F1 +40% and text-to-CT FID −75%.
papers.tags: Medical Medical AI 3D medical imaging CT VLM tokenizer wavelet causal convolution lookup-free quantization LFQ three-stage curriculum report generation text-to-CT CT-RATE RadChestCT LLaMA flow matching NeurIPS 2025

papers.title: Virtual colon flattening method based on colonic outer surface
papers.topic: Virtual Colonoscopy · Colon Flattening / Distortion Correction
papers.blurb: Switches the flattening distortion-correction benchmark from the rough inner colon surface to the smooth outer surface: uses the 'in-sphere range' of the geodesic distance field to cut colon-wall adhesions (OSE), and uses 'isodistant-surface velocity' to automatically split turns (DCA). On 60 CT cases, detection rate is 79.6% vs 67.1/71.9/72.7%, with false positives per case 0.16 vs 0.32/0.21/0.26.
papers.tags: Colonoscopy CTC Colon Flattening Medical virtual colonoscopy outer surface geodesic distortion correction SJTU Jun Zhao CMPB

papers.title: High-Performance CAD-CTC Scheme Using Shape Index, Multiscale Enhancement Filters, and Radiomic Features
papers.blurb: Moves Shape Index and dot/line/plane multiscale enhancement filters onto a 'Gaussian-smoothed geodesic distance field (GDF)' to generate polyp candidates, then uses 640-dimensional radiomics + Random Forests to reduce false positives. ≥5mm reaches 98.1% by-polyp / 95.3% per-scan @1.3 FP/case; changing the coordinate system reduces candidate FP from 104.5 to 58.8.
papers.tags: Colonoscopy CTC CAD polyp detection Medical radiomics geodesic distance field shape index random forests false positive reduction SJTU Jun Zhao IEEE TBME

papers.title: Virtual eversion and rotation of colon based on outer surface centerline
papers.blurb: Turns the colon 'inside out' (virtual eversion): the sampling centerline is changed from the jagged inner-surface centerline to the smooth outer-surface centerline, and eversion performs a distance-preserving reflection of the outer surface along sampling lines, E=2R-I. Failed eversion regions drop from 6.8→1.9 per case (71% fewer), eversion computation from 983s→75s (92% less), and reading time is about 77% shorter than VC.
